item.title: U.S.-Iran peace efforts inch along, mired by Israel's fight with Hezbollah
item.description: A long-term U.S.-Iran peace deal may depend on a separate agreement between Israel and Lebanon. Analysts say that presents a problem.

item.title: He dreamed of becoming a physician assistant. New loan rules may thwart him.
item.description: Starting July 1, the government will cap what graduate students may borrow in federal loans, forcing many toward private lenders with higher interest rates.

item.title: Maps show heat dome forecast to scorch major U.S. cities this week
item.description: A heat wave will blast much of the eastern U.S. this week, and forecasters say temperatures will feel even hotter because of the high humidity that's arriving with it.

item.title: D.C.'s July 4th fireworks will have "TSA-style" security
item.description: This year's Fourth of July celebrations in D.C. — marking the nation's 250th birthday — will include hours of military flyovers and a massive fireworks display that could stretch late into the night.

item.title: Body of suspected bear attack victim found on mountain
item.description: Fatal maulings in the last three months have jumped fivefold compared to last year, according to government data.

item.title: Democratic primaries in Colorado mark latest fight over party's direction
item.description: One week after Democratic insurgent victories in New York, the focus is now moving to Colorado, where challenges in a trio of races are threatening candidates backed by the party establishment.

item.title: GOP Rep. Tom Kean set to return to Congress today after mysterious illness
item.description: The New Jersey congressman missed more than 140 votes since March 5 as those around him declined to give specifics about his medical issue.

item.title: CBS Chicago journalists attacked by 3 men near Adler Planetarium
item.description: One of the men then smashed our photographer's camera while the other smashed the windshield of our news truck.

item.title: This number helps explain why many Americans are down on the economy
item.description: American workers' share of the nation's income is at its lowest point in almost 80 years, as more of the economy's gains flow to corporations and investors.

item.title: Ukrainian construction tycoon possibly targeted in Monaco explosion
item.description: French and Ukrainian media say an explosive device left in a Monaco apartment building seriously wounded construction magnate Vadym Iermolaiev and his wife.

item.title: Why some Europeans resist air conditioning amid deadly heat waves
item.description: Europe has the oldest population of any continent while also warming the fastest, leading to a dangerous and deadly situation.

item.title: Second Dan Sullivan can appear on Alaska Senate primary ballot, court rules
item.description: The Alaska Supreme Court ruled Monday that a man with the same name and party as Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan can challenge the sitting lawmaker in the state's Senate primary in August.

item.title: Putin admits Ukrainian strikes on infrastructure causing "problems" for Russia
item.description: Ukraine has intensified strikes on Russia recently, targeting energy and logistics infrastructure.

item.title: 6/29: CBS Evening News
item.description: High winds and temps continue to fuel major wildfire in Utah; JetBlue flight reports collision with drone while landing at JFK.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Colorado Supreme Court rejects efforts to put redistricting on the ballot
item.description: The Colorado Supreme Court on Monday delivered a death blow to ballot measures aimed at handing Democrats seven of Colorado's eight Congressional seats.

item.title: Staffer at Utah resort describes evacuation from wildfire
item.description: In southern Utah, the Cottonwood Fire tore through part of Eagle Point Resort, forcing it to close. Samantha Garcia, director of marketing for Eagle Point, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: NASA prepares for daring rescue to save aging telescope from falling to Earth
item.description: NASA will attempt a daring space rescue mission as soon as Tuesday morning to prevent an aging telescope from falling back to Earth. CBS News space consultant Bill Harwood has more.

item.title: Earthquakes exposed Venezuela's weak emergency medical system, New York Times reports
item.description: The back-to-back earthquakes in Venezuela last week knocked out power in two of the three public hospitals in the hard-hit coastal state of La Guaira, the director of the nonprofit "United Doctors of Venezuela" tells the New York Times. New York Times reporter Genevieve Glatsky joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Ship traffic drops in Strait of Hormuz after weekend attacks
item.description: According to maritime tracking website Kpler, 29 vessels passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday and 12 on Sunday, a sharp drop from the 74 ships on Wednesday. On Thursday, Iranian drones hit a ship in the strait, setting off retaliatory strikes from the United States. Over the weekend, Iran attacked a second ship and launched missile and drone strikes at American bases in the region. Aaron MacLean, CBS News national security analyst and host of the "School of War podcast," has the latest.

item.title: More than 1,300 heat-related deaths across Europe since June 21, WHO chief says
item.description: Temperatures broke records across Europe again on Sunday. The head of the World Health Organization says more than 1,300 heat-related deaths have been recorded since June 21. Leigh Kiniry has more from London.

item.title: What Americans think is best about the U.S., according to CBS News polling
item.description: As we approach 250 years of the nation's founding, new CBS News polling asked Americans about some of the best parts of living in the United States. CBS News' Anthony Salvanto has more.

item.title: Dangerous heat wave to hit parts of U.S. ahead of July 4
item.description: More than 100 record-high temperatures are expected through Saturday night in the eastern half of the country. Meteorologist Lonnie Quinn reports.

item.title: More mixed messages on U.S.-Iran peace talks
item.description: President Trump says the U.S. and Iran will resume talks in Doha on Tuesday, but Iran's senior negotiator denies a meeting is scheduled. CBS News' Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: Inside the Supreme Court's rulings on presidential firings, mail-in ballots
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday handed down decisions in cases that dealt with presidential firings and mail-in ballots. CBS News' Jessica Levinson has more.

item.title: Iran calls Strait of Hormuz situation "sensitive and complex" amid shaky ceasefire
item.description: Iran denied its negotiators would be meeting with U.S. officials in Qatar after President Trump announced the talks would resume at Tehran's request.

item.title: Carl Rinsch sentenced to 2.5 years in prison for defrauding Netflix
item.description: According to prosecutors and testimony, Carl Rinsch told Netflix he needed $11 million to finish a show, but spent whopping sums on luxury cars, watches and other goods, including $638,000 on two mattresses.

item.title: Iran war has cost Americans $1,000 per household, economist estimates
item.description: The Iran war has significantly driven up the cost of fuel, airfare and other U.S. goods, according to Moody's Analytics chief economist Mark Zandi.

item.title: The Trump administration says SNAP is rife with fraud and waste. Is it?
item.description: The USDA says almost 11% of SNAP payments contain errors, almost double the threshold set by Congress. Here's what is going on.

item.title: NASA prepares to launch rescue to save telescope from falling to Earth
item.description: The $30 million salvage operation gets underway as soon as this week with the planned launch of a robotic lifesaver.
